question: A basket contains 9 apples, 8 bananas and 7 oranges. A fruit is picked from the basket, find the probability that it is neither an apple nor an orange.
solution:
n(apples) = 9
n(bananas) = 8
n(oranges) = 7

n(ε) = 24

Hence Prob(not apple, nor orange) = Prob(banana) = 8/24
 = 1/3

question: The pie chart above shows the statistical distribution of 80 students in five subjects in an examination. Calculate how many student offer Mathematics.
solution:
5x° + (16x - 24)° + 5x° + (4x + 12)° + (6x + 12)° = 360°

36x° - 24 + 12 + 12 = 360°

36x° = 360°

x° = 360°/36


= 10°

Thus, the angle of sector representing Mathematics is 5 x 10° = 50°. Hence the number of students who offer mathematics is

50/(360) × 80 ≈ 11

question: In the diagram given, find the value of x.
solution:
In the diagram above, < CDE = < CED = 15°

(base < s of isos. △)

< ECD = 180° - (15 + 15)°

= 180° - 30° = 150°

But x + 110° = 150°

(Sum of opp. interior < s of a△
 = opp. exterior < )

x = 150° - 110° = 40°

question: If the angles of a quadrilateral are (3y + 10)°, (2y + 30)°, (y + 20)° and 4y°. Find the value of y.
solution:
Sum of angles in a quadrilateral = 360°

∴
 (3y + 10) + (2y + 30) + (y + 20) + 4y = 360

10y + 60 = 360 ⟹
 10y = 300

y = 30°

question: The mean of seven numbers is 10. If six of the numbers are 2, 4, 8, 14, 16 and 18, find the mode.
solution:
Using x = ∑x/N in each case, we get;

∑<sup>i=1</sup><sub>6</sub> x<sub>i</sub>
 = 10 x 7 = 70

∑<sup>i=1</sup><sub>7</sub>x<sub>i</sub>
 = 2 + 4 + 8 + 14 + 16 + 18 = 62

Hence the missing number can be obtained from

∑<sup>i=1</sup><sub>6</sub>x<sub>i</sub> − 
∑<sup>i=1</sup><sub>7</sub>x<sub>i</sub>
 = 70 - 62 = 8

So, all the seven numbers are 2, 4, 8, 8, 14, 16, 18

Mode = 8

question: The value x in the figure given is
What is the coordinate of centre of the circle X2+y2+2x-4y =10?
The graph above is correctly represented by
solution:
The graph crosses the x-axis at x = -1 and x = 2

Thus, x + 1 = 0 and x - 2 = 0

x<sup>2</sup> - 2x + x - 2 = 0

x<sup>2</sup> - x - 2 = 0

giving y =x<sup>2</sup> - x - 2

question: The remainder when 6p3 - p<sup>2</sup> - 47p + 30 is divided by p - 3 is
solution:
Let f(p) = 6p<sup>3</sup> - p<sup>2</sup> - 47p + 30

Then by the remainder theorem,

(p - 3): f(3) = remainder R,

i.e. f(3) = 6(3)<sup>3</sup> - (3)2 - 47(3) + 30 = R

162 - 9 - 141 + 30 = R

192 - 150 = R

R = 42

question: What is the 15th term of the sequence -3, 2,7,…?
If the variance of 3+x, 6, 4, x and 7-x is 4 and the mean is 5, find the standard deviation
solution:
Let δ<sup>2</sup> and δ denote the variance and standard deviation of the distribution respectively.

But δ<sup>2</sup> = 4 (given)

Hence δ = √4 = 2
